reasoning: Deporting a Lawful Permanent Resident via contested immigration proceedings typically takes 1 to 2 years at a minimum even when the individual is detained. Mahmoud Khalil was arrested in March 2025, and as of the knowledge cutoff (May 2025), he has multiple federal injunctions (from Judges Furman and Farbiarz) actively blocking his removal while constitutional challenges and habeas corpus petitions are heard. The deadline for this question is July 1, 2025, which gives a window of under two months (47 days) from the knowledge cutoff. In that timeframe, the government would have to win in federal court to lift the stays, defeat his asylum claim in immigration court, and prevail at the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA), which is structurally impossible given normal court scheduling and procedural rights. Thus, resolving the multi-tiered judicial review process and deporting him within this brief timeframe is highly improbable.
